perturbation.original_text: E. PAYMENT. Except as may be specifically authorized in writing by NETGEAR, and subject to NETGEAR's continuing approval of Distributor's credit status and financial condition, Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for the initial order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR.
perturbation.changed_text: E. PAYMENT. Except as may be specifically authorized in writing by NETGEAR, and subject to NETGEAR's continuing approval of Distributor's credit status and financial condition, Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within 15 days of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for the initial order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within 45 days of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying different payment terms for all orders versus the initial order. The first sentence mandates payment within 15 days for all orders, while the subsequent sentence extends the payment deadline to 45 days for the initial order. This contradicts which payment term should be enforced for the initial order, creating ambiguity and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. PAYMENT. Except as may be specifically authorized in writing by NETGEAR, and subject to NETGEAR's continuing approval of Distributor's credit status and financial condition, Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within 15 days of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for the initial order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within 45 days of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. NETGEAR, in its sole discretion, reserves the right to specify, and to change from time to time, Distributor's credit line and payment terms. All payments are to be made in U.S. dollars. Payment for shipments made outside of the United States shall be made by wire transfer in accordance with wire transfer procedures provided by NETGEAR. If at any time Distributor is delinquent in the payment of any invoice, exceeds the credit line established by NETGEAR, or is otherwise in breach of the Agreement, NETGEAR may, in its discretion, withhold shipment (including partial shipments) of any order or may require Distributor to pay cash on delivery for further shipments. Payment not received by NETGEAR when due may be subject to a late payment INGRAM NETGEAR Distributor Agreement 3 083096 service charge provided NETGEAR has provided 10 days written notice to Distributor of Distributor's failure to pay. The foregoing notwithstanding, Distributor shall not be deemed in default under this provision if Distributor withholds payment of amounts legitimately in dispute on any invoice provided that (1) Distributor promptly pays the undisputed portion of the invoice in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (2) Distributor provides NETGEAR with written notice of the disputed amount within 10 days of receipt of the invoice; and (3) Distributor works in good faith with NETGEAR to resolve any dispute within a reasonable time period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The first sentence of Section E states payment is due within 15 days for all products, while the second sentence specifies 45 days for the initial order. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, demonstrates this contradiction within the same section. This ambiguity regarding the payment deadline for the initial order creates an in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: B. CANCELLATION AND RESCHEDULING. Distributor may not cancel or reschedule any order, in whole or in part, less than five business days prior to the corresponding shipment date specified in NETGEAR's order acknowledgment.
perturbation.changed_text: B. CANCELLATION AND RESCHEDULING. Distributor may not cancel any order once it has been acknowledged by NETGEAR. Rescheduling of orders is permitted up to two business days prior to the corresponding shipment date specified in NETGEAR's order acknowledgment, subject to a 10% rescheduling fee.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. First sentence completely prohibits cancellations, creating the potential to create a legal issue. The second sentence allows rescheduling with a fee, suggesting that cancelling is not entirely restricted but rather discouraged.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. CANCELLATION AND RESCHEDULING. Distributor may not cancel any order once it has been acknowledged by NETGEAR. Rescheduling of orders is permitted up to two business days prior to the corresponding shipment date specified in NETGEAR's order acknowledgment, subject to a 10% rescheduling fee. C. RETURNS. Products received by Distributor as a result of an error by NETGEAR in shipment may be returned for credit. Such credit will include an amount equal to the purchase price of the Product shipped in error as well as the cost of return freight paid by Distributor to return the Product to NETGEAR. Products with defects covered by the warranty may be returned for remedy under the warranty. Prior to returning any Product, whether for exchange or warranty or non-warranty action, Distributor must obtain a Return Materials Authorization (RMA) number from NETGEAR. Distributor should return the Product to NETGEAR, with shipping charges prepaid. NETGEAR will not accept collect shipments. Any Product returned to NETGEAR, which is not returned in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, may be refused.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed cancellation or rescheduling under certain conditions. The changed text initially states that cancellation is prohibited once an order is acknowledged, but then immediately contradicts itself by allowing rescheduling with a fee. This contradicts the contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text in this instance. Even though the contradicted and changed text are identical, the change from the original text introduced a strong contradiction within the document, regarding the cancellation and rescheduling policy.

perturbation.original_text: B. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if (i) the other party becomes insolvent, files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy, or ceases doing business; or (ii) the other party fails to cure a material breach of the Agreement within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of such breach from the party not in default. Upon termination of the Agreement by NETGEAR for Distributor's breach, NETGEAR may cancel all of Distributor's unfulfilled orders without further obligation. This Agreement may be terminated at any time without cause by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: B. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if (i) the other party becomes insolvent, files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy, or ceases doing business; or (ii) the other party fails to cure a material breach of the Agreement within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of such breach from the party not in default. Upon termination of the Agreement by NETGEAR for Distributor's breach, NETGEAR may cancel all of Distributor's unfulfilled orders without further obligation. This Agreement may be terminated at any time without cause by NETGEAR only upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by restricting which party can terminate the contract at any given time without a valid reason, in this perturbation the NETGEAR entity. However in the first sentence, it states that both entities can terminate with valid reasoning, but the change now restricts the option to only NETGEAR being allowed to do so, creating a contradiction and uncertainty in contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if (i) the other party becomes insolvent, files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy, or ceases doing business; or (ii) the other party fails to cure a material breach of the Agreement within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of such breach from the party not in default. Upon termination of the Agreement by NETGEAR for Distributor's breach, NETGEAR may cancel all of Distributor's unfulfilled orders without further obligation. This Agreement may be terminated at any time without cause by NETGEAR only upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party. C. EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Except as otherwise specifically stated in the Agreement, neither party will be liable to the other for damages in any form by reason of the expiration or earlier termination of the Agreement. D. CONTINUING EFFECT. Any expiration or earlier termination of the Agreement does not modify or alter any of the obligations of the parties which accrued prior to such expiration or termination. The sections of the Agreement which address taxes; duty; fee; payment; security interest; proprietary rights and information; warranties; foreign reshipment; remedies; limitations; termination and governing law survive any expiration or termination of the Agreement. The section entitled SOFTWARE LICENSES also survives any expiration or termination provided Distributor and end-users continues to comply with the provisions of the applicable software license terms. Except as expressly agreed in writing between the parties, no party is liable to the other for any dollar amounts, costs or damages by reason of the expiration or earlier termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are the same. The change restricts the termination clause, previously available to both parties, to NETGEAR only. This directly contradicts the initial statement allowing 'either party' to terminate without cause, creating a clear inconsistency regarding the termination rights within the agreement as the definition provided states: Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.
